task_completion.description: Log in to the OpenMRS Administration interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS Administration dashboard is visible in the browser, indicating successful login (typically shows a header with 'OpenMRS' and various administrative links like 'Manage Encounters').

task_completion.description: Navigate to the encounter management or search section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page titled 'Manage Encounters', 'Find Encounters', or similar is visible within the OpenMRS Administration interface.

task_completion.description: Locate the voided 'Vitals' encounter for Sarah Johnson.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Search results or an encounter details page for patient 'Sarah Johnson' (or identifier BAH000004) is shown. The encounter type must be 'Vitals' and there must be a visual indication that it is voided (e.g., a 'voided' status, a strikethrough, or an 'Unvoid'/'Restore' button present).

A success message indicating the encounter was restored/unvoided, or the encounter details page showing it is no longer voided (e.g., an active 'Void this Encounter' button is now visible instead of 'Unvoid').

task_completion.description: Verify the encounter is active and visible in the standard patient dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The standard OpenMRS or Bahmni patient clinical dashboard for Sarah Johnson is open, and the 'Vitals' encounter from today is actively listed in the recent visits, observations, or encounters section.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser GUI to interact with OpenMRS.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenMRS web interface, rather than a terminal executing curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent actually navigated through the OpenMRS interface to restore the encounter.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate steps like the admin dashboard, encounter search, and patient dashboard are visible across the screenshots, not just a final mocked-up screen.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a database client or terminal to run SQL queries to unvoid the encounter.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running MySQL/PostgreSQL commands; all restoration actions are performed within the OpenMRS web interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient name is 'Sarah Johnson'. The encounter type to restore is 'Vitals'. Note: The task description mentions identifier BAH000003, but the actual identifier in the system for Sarah Johnson is BAH000004. The agent should successfully restore the Vitals encounter for Sarah Johnson regardless of the identifier discrepancy.
